ks1.objective: Work together as a team whilst avoiding taggers and helping others stay in the game.
successCriteria: Moves into space to avoid taggers

lks2.objective: Develop agility, awareness and teamwork whilst competing in a tag game.
successCriteria: Avoids taggers using changes of direction

uks2.objective: Use teamwork, movement and awareness to outplay the tagging team.

space.description: Create a rectangular playing area. One team begins as taggers whilst the other two teams move freely around the area.

value.name: Positivity
value.text: Positivity means encouraging teammates, staying enthusiastic and responding well when things become challenging. Players should celebrate effort and teamwork throughout the activity.

steps: The other two teams move around the area avoiding being tagged.
If tagged, players complete a challenge before returning to the game.
Award points for successful tags and surviving players.
progressions: Require players to perform a ball mastery skill before rejoining.
Allow players to rescue teammates by completing a challenge together.
questions: How did you avoid being tagged?
How did your team help each other?
What movement made it hardest for taggers to catch you?

step.space: Reduce the area to increase pressure or increase it to support success.
step.task: Add footballs and require dribbling throughout.
step.equipment: Use coloured cones and introduce safe zones.
step.people: Adjust the number of taggers to balance the challenge.

base.showmanship: Create team identities, encourage celebrations and keep score between rounds. Build excitement by announcing team challenges and rewarding teamwork.
